def: s.m. The red species of the Lodh tree, Symplocos racemosa (the bark of which is used in dying, and as an astringent).
s.m. A spear with a sharp edge.
s.m. A person armed with a paṭṭiś or spear.
adv. see s.v. paṭṭī. s.m. Pedestrian; foot-soldier; — s.f. The smallest division of an army, company, platoon (consisting of one chariot, one elephant, three horsemen, and five foot-soldiers)
s.f. A small leaf, leaflet; a letter; written opinion given by Pandits on a point of Hindū law.
s.m. Preservative from sin; preserver, saviour.
s.m. Leaf, &c. ( = patra, pattar, q.v.); almanac, ephemeris, calendar.

s.m. Wing of a bird, pinion, feather, feather of an arrow; any vehicle (e.g. chariot, cart, horse, camel, &c.); leaf (of a tree, or of a flower, or of a book); leaf of the Laurus cassia (syn. tej-pattā); a leaf prepared for writing on, paper; letter, epistle, document, deed; gold leaf, &c., a thin piece, or plate, of metal; a grant or conveyance of land engraven on a plate of metal; lines and signs painted on the face with musk and other fragrant substances
subs: (patra+aṅka), s.m. The figure or number of the page of a book, paging
patrāṅg (patra+aṅga), s.m. Red sanders, Pterocarpus santolinus; red or sappan wood; a species of birch
s.m. A species of plant, a red sort of Tulsī or basil, Ocymum sanctum
s.m. A species of birch (from the bark of which ḥuqqa snakes, &c. are made)
s.m. Embellishing a page, illuminating, gilding
patra-nāṛikā, s.f. The vein or fibre of a leaf
(patra+ūr˚), s.m. The plant Calosanthes Indica; and Bignonia Indica.

def: s.f. (m. -twa), The quality of preserving from sin; the power or function of a preserver.
s.m. A kind of coarse woollen cloth.
adj., see پٿو paṭu.
adj. Pungent, hot, acid; sharp, clever, smart, dextrous, skilful; crafty, sly, cunning; clear; intellectual; diligent; harsh, hard, cruel.
s.m. One who strings beads, pearls, &c.; a maker of braid, fringe, tape, &c. (syn. ʻalāqa-band).
s.m. The plant Hibiscus cannabinus, and the fruit of the same (commonly called sorrel); tow made from the Hibiscus cannabinus.
adj. & s.m. Falling frequently or habitually, apt or disposed to fall; falling, or losing caste; — declivity of a mountain, precipice; — an aquatic animal of a large size; (fig.) the water-elephant.
s.m. = paṭkā, q.v.
s.f. Small pot, pan (cf. patīlī); a small earthen vessel.
s.m. A ferryman.
s.f. Harlot, &c. ( = pātur); — s.m. Whoremonger ( = pātur-bāz).
s.f. = paturiyā, pātur, q.v. s.f. Harlot, &c. ( = pātur); — s.m. Whoremonger ( = pātur-bāz).
s.m. = H پٿوتا पटूता paṭūtā, s.f. = paṭutā, q.v.
s.f. = H پٿوت पटूत paṭūt, s.m. = paṭutā, q.v.
s.f. = S پٿتو पटुत्व paṭutva, s.m. (from paṭu), Sharpness, keenness, cleverness, dexterity, expertness, skill, vigilance, activity; vigour, virility; eloquence.
s.m. = S پٿتا पटुता paṭutā, s.f. (from paṭu), Sharpness, keenness, cleverness, dexterity, expertness, skill, vigilance, activity; vigour, virility; eloquence.
s.f. Rudder, helm, a large oar or paddle used as a rudder.
v.t. To cause to be roofed, &c., to have (a house) roofed, &c.; to have (a field) irrigated, to water ( = paṭāna); to provide money, &c. (see paṭnā).
s.f. = pati-vatnī, q.v.s.v. pati.
s.f. Rudder, helm, a large oar or paddle used as a rudder.
s.m. One who keeps all the accounts connected with the lands of one or more villages; village registrar or accountant, land-steward
subs: s.f. The office or business of a paṭwārī.

def: s.m. One who keeps all the accounts connected with the lands of one or more villages; village registrar or accountant, land-steward
subs: s.f. The office or business of a paṭwārī.
wety: Inherited from Sanskrit *पट्टपालिन् (*paṭṭapālin, literally “slab-keeper, cloth-keeper”). Equivalent to پَٹْوار (paṭvār) + ـی (-ī).

def: s.m. A perch or roost for a falcon or hawk.
s.f. Fringe, braid, tape, &c.
s.f. Place for watering cattle; a shed on the road-side for accommodating travellers with water, place where water is distributed, a drinking fount ( = pauh, q.v.)
s.f. The one or ace on dice
subs: s.f. The ace and the twelve in dice; (fig.) good luck, good fortune, success; — pau-bārah paṛnā (-ko), To have a stroke of fortune, to come off well or successful

def: s.f. Young plants (for transplantation); — offspring, children, progeny.
s.m. A young plant, plant, sapling; a shrub, bush; — a child, young boy or girl; — a tassel.
wety: Deaspirated variant of پَودھا (paudhā), from Sanskrit प्रवृद्ध (pravṛddha).

def: s.m. A plant = paudā, q.v.
subs: s.f. A nursery for plants. s.m. A young plant, plant, sapling; a shrub, bush; — a child, young boy or girl; — a tassel.

def: s.m. See paud and pauda.
s.m. See paud and pauda.
s.m. Ground on which plants are sown for transplantation, a nursery for plants.

s.m. A stand where water is kept for travellers, &c. ( = pau, q.v.). s.f. Place for watering cattle; a shed on the road-side for accommodating travellers with water, place where water is distributed, a drinking fount ( = pauh, q.v.)
s.f. Gate, &c. ( = paur, paurī, paul); threshold, entrance.
s.m. Door-keeper, gate-keeper, porter ( = pauriyā).
s.m. A pound (weight).

adj. & s.m. A quarter less, one fourth less (than one or any other number), three quarters; — the three-fourths part; — paunā, s.m. A multiplication table of which the multiplier is three-fourths; — paune, adj. = paun
subs: adj. A quarter less than two, one and three-quarters
adj. A fourth less than three, two and three-quarters
adj. A hundred diminished by the fourth part, seventy-five
adj. Two hundred diminished by the fourth of a hundred, one hundred and seventy-five.

def: v.t. (prov.), To eat.

s.m. = paunā, 'ladle,' q.v. adj. & s.m. A quarter less, one fourth less (than one or any other number), three quarters; — the three-fourths part; — paunā, s.m. A multiplication table of which the multiplier is three-fourths; — paune, adj. = paun
subs: adj. A quarter less than two, one and three-quarters
adj. A fourth less than three, two and three-quarters
adj. A hundred diminished by the fourth part, seventy-five
adj. Two hundred diminished by the fourth of a hundred, one hundred and seventy-five.

def: adj. & s.m. A quarter less, one fourth less (than one or any other number), three quarters; — the three-fourths part; — paunā, s.m. A multiplication table of which the multiplier is three-fourths; — paune, adj. = paun
subs: adj. A quarter less than two, one and three-quarters
adj. A fourth less than three, two and three-quarters
adj. A hundred diminished by the fourth part, seventy-five
adj. Two hundred diminished by the fourth of a hundred, one hundred and seventy-five.

def: s.m. A perforated iron ladle or spoon used (esp. by confectioners) for skimming or straining; strainer, colander.
s.m. The son of a twice-married woman by her second husband (one of the sons or heirs admitted by the old Hindū law).
s.m. Repetition,tautology.
s.m. Repetition, tautology.
s.f. Corr. of pahuṅć, q.v.
s.m. Five and a half (in surveying); the multiplication table of five and a half times.
v.n. = pahuṅćnā, q.v. v.n. To arrive, reach, come (upon), come (to or up to); to accrue, extend, befall; to have access (to).
wety: Inherited from Sanskrit प्रोञ्छति (proñchati). Cognate with Marathi पुसणे (pusṇe), Assamese মচা (mosa).

def: s.m. = paunḍra, paunḍrak, q.v. s.m. A species of sugar-cane of a pale straw colour; epithet of a particular mixed caste of hereditary sugar-boilers (the son of a Vaiśya by a woman of the distiller class
subs: or paunḍraka-vardhan, s.m. Name of the province of Bihār in Central India.

def: s.m. A red variety of the common sugar-cane, Saccharum officiarum; also a pale straw-coloured variety of sugar-cane.
v.n. To lie down, &c. = pauṛhnā, q.v. v.n. To lie down, repose, rest ( = paṛnā).
s.m. A species of sugar-cane of a pale straw colour; epithet of a particular mixed caste of hereditary sugar-boilers (the son of a Vaiśya by a woman of the distiller class
subs: or paunḍraka-vardhan, s.m. Name of the province of Bihār in Central India.

def: s.m. A species of sugar-cane of a pale straw colour; epithet of a particular mixed caste of hereditary sugar-boilers (the son of a Vaiśya by a woman of the distiller class
subs: or paunḍraka-vardhan, s.m. Name of the province of Bihār in Central India.

def: see s.v. paun, paunā. s.m. A pound (weight).
s.f. = pahuṅćī, q.v. s.f. An ornament worn on the wrist, a bracelet (esp. a bracelet of gold beads strung together and fastened with a clasp).
s.m. = pahuṅćā, q.v. s.m. The fore-arm, the wrist.
v.n. = pahuṅćnā, q.v. v.n. To arrive, reach, come (upon), come (to or up to); to accrue, extend, befall; to have access (to).
s.f. The recipients of presents at marriages, &c.; people of low caste (such as barbers, shoe-makers, &c.).

s.m. = pau-sarā, q.v.s.v. pau. s.m. = pau-sālā, q.v.s.v. pau. s.f. See s.v. pau. s.f. Place for watering cattle; a shed on the road-side for accommodating travellers with water, place where water is distributed, a drinking fount ( = pauh, q.v.)
s.m. = puṅ-savan, q.v.s.v. puṅ. s.m. See s.v. puṅ. s.m. Male, male being, man (used chiefly in comp.)
subs: adj. & s.m. Bringing forth a male; causing the birth of a male child; — the first of the essential ceremonies of Hindū initiation, a religious and domestic festival held on the mother's perceiving the first signs of a living conception
s.f. Manly vigour, manliness, manhood, virility
adj. & s.m., Chief, best, most excellent ( = śreshṭh); a distinguished or eminent person, a hero
vulg. pul-ling, s.m. The characteristic sign of a man, manhood, virility; the male organ; (in Gram.) the masculine gender
s.m. 'An elephant of a man,' a distinguished or preëminent man; — a white elephant; — the tree Rottleria tinctoria (from the blossoms of which a yellowish dye is prepared); — a white lotus; — nutmeg
s.m. A nakshatra or asterism regarded as a male; an asterism under which males are procreated.

def: s.m. = puṅś-ćalya, q.v.s.v. puṅs.
s.f. Town-duty.
s.f. Gate, door ( = paulī).
adj. & s.m. Of or belonging to a town or city; town-bred; produced or manufactured in a town or city, town-made; — a townsman, citizen; a species of fragrant grass
subs: s.m. Fellow-citizen-ship, connection or equality with others formed by inhabiting the same city for ten years.

def: adj. & s.m. Belonging or relating to the past or to past ages; familiar with the events or legends of the past; — relating to or belonging to the Purāṇas; — a Brāhmaṇ well-read in the Purāṇas, a mythologist.

adj. & s.m. = paurāṇik, q.v. adj. & s.m. Belonging or relating to the past or to past ages; familiar with the events or legends of the past; — relating to or belonging to the Purāṇas; — a Brāhmaṇ well-read in the Purāṇas, a mythologist.
s.f. = pūrṇa-māsī, q.v.s.v. pūrṇa.
adj. Belonging to or descended from Pūru.
v.t. To cause to lie down, to put to sleep; to lay prostrate.
v.n. To lie down, repose, rest ( = paṛnā).
s.f. A gate, door ( = paur, q.v.). s.f. Gate, door ( = paulī).
s.m. Door-keeper, gate-keeper, porter, janitor ( = paulīyā).
s.m. The office, character or functions of a purohit or family priest.

adj. & s.m. (f. -ī), Belonging or relating to a man or to man in general; manly, virile, human; being of the length of a man with both arms elevated and the fingers extended; — the measure of a man, or the height to which he reaches with both arms elevated and the fingers extended; — a weight or load which can be carried by one man; — manhood, manliness, manly strength or courage, heroism, virility, strength, power, vigour; human action, action of men, man's work, action; effort, exertion, endeavour; — semen virile; membrum virile
subs: v.n. To exert vigour, to exert oneself, &c.

def: s.f. = S پورشتو पौरुषत्व paurushatva, s.m. Manliness, manly strength or spirit, &c. ( = paurush)
s.m. = S پورشتا पौरुषता paurushatā, s.f. Manliness, manly strength or spirit, &c. ( = paurush)
adj. Derived from or relating to man, incidental to man; human, manly, male, virile; coming from the soul, spiritual.
s.f. The measure of a man ( = paurush).
adj. Manly; masculine, male.
adj. = pūrva-dehik, q.v.s.v. pūrva.
adj. Belonging to former times, prior, former, previous, primary, old.
s.f. See s.v. pau. s.f. Place for watering cattle; a shed on the road-side for accommodating travellers with water, place where water is distributed, a drinking fount ( = pauh, q.v.)

s.m. = pau-sālā, q.v.s.v. pau. s.f. See s.v. pau. s.f. Place for watering cattle; a shed on the road-side for accommodating travellers with water, place where water is distributed, a drinking fount ( = pauh, q.v.)
s.m. = pūs, q.v. s.m. The ninth solar month of the Hindūs (Dec.-Jan., the full moon of which is near pushya, three stars in Cancer).
s.f. The night or day of full moon in the month paush or pūs (Dec.-Jan.).
s.f. A lotus-pool; a large pond, reservoir.
s.m. The twenty-eighth nakshatra or lunar mansion.
adj. Relating to or belonging to flowers; made of flowers; flowery, floral.
s.m. Oxide of brass (used as a collyrium), green vitriol.
adv. Relating to or promoting growth or welfare; nutritious, nourishing, nutritive, fattening, invigorating; preservative, protective.
s.f. A kind of lamp.

s.m. A grandson ( = potā, q.v.). s.m. The young of any animal; a young elephant ten years old; a young plant or tree; the young shoot of a plant; a fetus which has no enveloping membrane; cloth, a garment; the site or foundation of a house; a vessel, ship, boat
subs: potaj, s.m. An animal which is produced or developed from a fetus which has no enveloping membrane (as an elephant and certain other quadrupeds, which are supposed, in the physiology of the Hindūs, to carry their young without the aid of an ovarium or uterus).

def: s.f. A son's daughter, granddaughter.
adj. Of or belonging to a son or to a grandson.
s.m. An image-worshipper, idolater.
s.m. = پوا pawwā, q.v. s.m. A weight or measure of a quarter of a ser; a quarter; a quart measure.
s.f. Chain for the leg, fetter, stocks; — the leg of a boot or stocking; an odd shoe or slipper.
s.m. Mean people. &c. (see pājī).

adj. & s.m. Cleansing, purifying; purificatory; pertaining to Agni; pure; — Agni, the deified personification of fire; fire (in general); a saint or person purified by religious abstraction; — a species of tree, Premna integrifolia or spinosa (the wood of which is used to procure fire by attrition); a species of plant, leadwort, Plumbago zeylanica; the plant Semecarpus anacardium, a plant used as a vermifuge, Carthamus tinctorius.
adj. Purifying, purificatory.
s.m. Wind, air; — name of a particular fire (regarded as a son of Agni); — an epithet of the moon.

adj. & s.m. Purifying, purificatory, purgatory, freeing from sin, sanctifying; purified, pure, holy; — purifier; fire (as purifying or cleansing); water; cowdung; incense; austere devotion, penance, expiation; — the seed of the plant Elœocarpus ganitus (of which rosaries are made); a species of grass, Costus speciosus

s.m.f. The act of purifying, purification; winnowing; 'the purifier,' wind, air; the Regent of the winds and of the north-west quarter; the wind of the body; the breath of life; — a householder's sacred fire; — spell, charm; the evil spirits appointed by a wizard or magician to injure any one
subs: s.m. Taking the air, walking or riding for diversion or exercise ( = hawā khānā)
or paun-pānī, s.m. 'Wind and water,' climate ( = āb o hawā)
s.f. Ascertaining the direction of the wind
or paun-sūt, s.m. = pavan-kā pūt, s.m. The son of Rāvaṇ, the ape Hanumān
v.t. To enchant; to injure by the aid of malignant spirits
s.m. An arrow projected by means of air; an arrow which when projected raises the wind
s.m. Morbid condition of wind; rheumatism, &c.

def: s.m. A species of grain, Andropogon saccharatus ( = yavanāl).
s.m. = paṅwāṛā q.v.
s.m. See s.v. pa. adj. & s.m. (f. pā), Drinking; drinker (used as last member of comp., e.g. dwi-pa, s.m. lit. 'Twice-drinking'; the elephant).
s.m. The rainy season; rain.
s.m. The act of sowing seed with the hand.
adj. & s.m. Purifying, purificatory; — who or what purifies, purifier.
s.m. The tire of a wheel; the metallic point of a spear or arrow; — the thunderbolt of Indra.
s.f. A dike or channel (for letting water in or out).
part. Purified.
part. Purified, cleansed.

adj. & s.m. = pavitra, q.v. s.m. Means of purification; kuśa grass, sacrificial grass, Poa cynosuroides; a purifying prayer or mantra; the sacred thread, Brāhmanical cord; — any divinity, as Agni, &c.

s.f. A holy woman; — holy basn, Ocymum sanctum; name of a river (the Pabar, a little to the north-west of Hardwār); the twelfth day of the light half of the month Śrāvan (on which a festival is observed in honour of Vishṇu).
s.m. A means of purification; a knotted piece of kuśa grass (used by the Hindūs in purification); the Brāhmanical thread; a string of silken beads.
s.m. Means of purification; kuśa grass, sacrificial grass, Poa cynosuroides; a purifying prayer or mantra; the sacred thread, Brāhmanical cord; — any divinity, as Agni, &c.
adj. Purifying, cleansing; morally or physically pure, holy, sinless, free from sensual propensities, undefiled, clean, nice
s.f. = H پوترتائي पवित्रताई pavitratāʼī, s.f. = S پوترتو पवित्रत्व pavitratva, s.m. Moral or physical purity, holiness, sanctity, purity, cleanness.
s.f. = S پوترتا पवित्रता pavitratā, s.f. = S پوترتو पवित्रत्व pavitratva, s.m. Moral or physical purity, holiness, sanctity, purity, cleanness.
s.m. = S پوترتا पवित्रता pavitratā, s.f. = H پوترتائي पवित्रताई pavitratāʼī, s.f. Moral or physical purity, holiness, sanctity, purity, cleanness.
s.f. A ring of kuśa grass, or of gold, silver, or copper worn on the ring finger and the forefinger by Hindūs during religious worship.
s.m. A weight or measure of a quarter of a ser; a quarter; a quart measure.
s.f. Fault, defect, blemish, vice.
s.m. = پايه pāya, q.v.
s.m. Leg or foot (of an animate or an inanimate object, as a table, bedstead, &c.); foundation (of a building); base (of a column, &c.); prop, support, stay; step; rank, dignity, degree.
s.m. Milk, water, juice, anything to drink
subs: payad, adj. & s.m. Yielding milk or water; — the female breast or nipple; a cloud
(or pay-) nidhī, s.m. (Myth.) The ocean of milk; the ocean ( = payo-nidhī, q.v.s.v payas).

def: adj. See s.v. pā. adj. & s.m. (f. pā), Drinking; drinker (used as last member of comp., e.g. dwi-pa, s.m. lit. 'Twice-drinking'; the elephant).
s.m. = payas, q.v. s.m. A dish made of rice, milk, and sugar (syn. khīr); an oblation of rice, milk, and sugar; — the resin of Pinus longifolia, turpentine.
adj. & s.m. Drinking; — a drinker.
s.f. Straw; long grass (used for thatching).
adj. & s.f. Sure-footed; easy-paced (elephant); — an anklet of gold or silver; a child born feet-foremost; a bamboo ladder.
wety: Inherited from Sanskrit *पादल (*pādala). Cognate with Gujarati પાળું (pāḷũ).

def: s.m. = paig̠ām, q.v.
s.m. = paig̠am-bar, q.v.s.v. paig̠am
s.m. Going forth, going to a distance, setting out, departure; death
subs: v.n. To go forth or away, to set out, to depart.

def: s.m. contrac. of pāyān, q.v.
End, extremity, extreme; termination, completion.
s.m. = payān, q.v. End, extremity, extreme; termination, completion.
adj. & s.m. Pertaining to departure or setting out; — going; journey, march.
adj. Lasting, durable, permanent, stable, firm.
End, extremity, extreme; termination, completion.
s.m. A species of grass (used for thatching, &c. = payāl, q.v.); — metre, a measure of verse consisting of two lines.
wety: Inherited from Middle Hindi پیار (pyar) (c. 1611), Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀧𑀺𑀆𑀭 (piāra), from Sanskrit प्रियकार (priyakāra), compound of प्रिय (priyá) + कार (kāra).

def: s.m. A dish made of rice, milk, and sugar (syn. khīr); an oblation of rice, milk, and sugar; — the resin of Pinus longifolia, turpentine.
s.m. Juice; water; milk
subs: adj. (f. -ā), Yielding milk; — s.m. 'Water-giver,' a cloud
s.m. lit. 'Water-holder'; or 'containing milk'; a cloud; a woman's breast; an udder; — the root of Scirpus kysoor; — a species of sugar-cane; — the cocoa-nut; — the fragrant grass Cyperus rotundus
adj. Having milk on the surface; milk-faced
s.m. 'Receptacle of water'; the ocean; a cloud; pond, lake

s.m. The act of subsisting on mere milk in consequence of a vow (which, with prayer and residence in a cow-house, for the period of one month, is regarded as an expiation for receiving an unsuitable present); — the offering milk to Vishṇu and subsisting upon it for twelve days as an act of merit; also for one day, or for three days.

def: adj. Rich in milk, yielding milk abundantly.
s.f. A cow with abundance of milk, a milch-cow.
s.f. Coagulated milk, curds (made by mixing sour with hot sweet milk); — the shrub Asclepias rosea; — a medicinal kind of moon-plant.
s.m. Scaffolding, scaffold (syn. maćān; ḍauṅjā).
s.m. Footman; foot-soldier; armed attendant; inferior police or revenue officer; village watchman; runner, messenger, harbinger.
s.f. = pāʼeṅtī, pāʼīṅtī, q.v. s.f. Foot or bottom of a bed ( = pāṅyatī).
s.m. Autumn.

see s.v. payas for compounds of which the first member is payo. s.m. A dish made of rice, milk, and sugar (syn. khīr); an oblation of rice, milk, and sugar; — the resin of Pinus longifolia, turpentine.
s.m. The tree Acacia catechu.
Cooking, baking; concocting (used in comp.).
s.m. Bezoar stone = pād-zahr, q.v. s.m. Antidote, Bezoar stone.
s.f. A brick-kiln.
s.m. See s.v. pā. adj. & s.m. (f. pā), Drinking; drinker (used as last member of comp., e.g. dwi-pa, s.m. lit. 'Twice-drinking'; the elephant).
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian پا زیب (pāzīb, pāzeb, literally “foot adornment”). First attested in c. 1713.

ety: perf. part. of pazh-murdan; and = Zend paiti+mereta, rt. mar; S. prati+mr̤ita, rt. mr̤ī
def: part. adj. Withered, faded, pallid, drooping, blighted, decayed; frozen, numbed.
s.f. Witheredness; fadedness; rottenness; frozen state, numbness; dejection (of spirits).

part. adj. (from paẕīruftan, 'to take,' &c., see next), Taking, receiving, accepting, admitting; being possessed of or endowed with, capable (of), susceptible, liable (used as the last member of compounds, e.g. iṣlāḥ-paẕīr, adj. Capable of correction or amendment, remediable).

ety: fr. paẕīr, imperat. of paẕīruftan, paẕī = Zend paiti = S. prati; ruftan, fr. rt. Zend ḥarp = S. sarp
def: adj. Acceptable, agreeable; accepted (a prayer or petition); — s.m. Acceptance.

ety: fr. paẕīr, imperat. of paẕīruftan, paẕī = Zend paiti = S. prati; ruftan, fr. rt. Zend ḥarp = S. sarp
def: adj. Acceptable, agreeable; accepted (a prayer or petition); — s.m. Acceptance.
s.f. Acceptance.
s.f. (used in comp.), Taking, acceptance.

r: pe, pa, Nāgarī, p, fe, fārsī, pārsī, b, poṅgā, boṅgā, upṭan, ubṭan, abjad, be, pa-kār
ety: It does not occur in the Arabic alphabet; but the letter fe is substituted for it in Persian words adopted into the Arabic language, thus fārsī for pārsī
def: s.m. The letter or sound pa
subs: vulg. pa-barg, s.m. The p series, the labial series of consonants.

ety: Prk. पले or पलि, or परि; S. परे; and परं
def: conj. On, upon, &c. = par, q.v. s.m. & adv. (gov. gen. with -ke, if the postp. is expressed), Further bank, or opposite shore or side (of a river, lake, &c.); the other side, concluding bound; the end or limit of anything, termination, conclusion; utmost limit or extent or reach; bottom, innermost depth, inmost recess; first turning up or ploughing of land; — over, across, on or to the other or further side, through, beyond
subs: v.t. To put or take across, to put or ferry over; to carry through, cause to reach the end, &c. (see next)
v.n. To go or get across (esp. the ocean of existence), to cross; to reach the end; to accomplish, get through; to succeed, gain one's end
v.n. To get to the bottom (of), to fathom, to sound
v.n. To come to an end, be over

v.t. To cross over, to ferry across or over; to pierce, go through and through, transfix; to carry through, finish, accomplish, get to the end (of, -ke); to relieve; to succeed, win, gain (in gambling)

pārag, adj. & s.m. Going to the opposite shore, crossing over, going through or over or across or to the end; intending to cross; completely mastering, thoroughly well versed in, knowing, clever, skilful, expert, able; — one who has arrived at the end, &c.

part. adj. & s.m. Passed over in safety, crossed; passed beyond the world; pure, holy; — one who has reached the opposite shore; one who has passed beyond the world; (with Jains) an Arhat or deified saint or teacher

pār honā or ho-jānā, v.n. To be carried across, to reach the shore; to be completed, finished, or accomplished; to go through and through, penetrate, permeate; to get well through, be successful, to succeed; to survive; to go away, be off, escape, attain refuge
and wār-pār, adv. On both sides (of a river, &c.); from end to end, through and through, quite through.

def: s.m. Turn, winding; revolution; involution; convolution; — twist, coil; plait, fold; — entanglement, complication, maze, perplexity, intricacy, ambiguity; hitch, difficulty, trouble; trick (in wrestling, &c.); artifice, stratagem; wiliness, craftiness; duplicity, deceit; — a roll (of cloth, &c.); a screw; a machine
subs: s.m. & adj. Twist upon twist, all convolution; all intricacy, &c.; — involved, intricate, &c.
v.n. To experience trouble, suffer distress or loss
v.n. The twist (of anything) to become loose; a screw to come out
(-meṅ), A difficulty or hitch to arise (in); to occur as a difficulty or obstacle; to become complicated, difficult, or intricate; — to be entangled (in)
v.n. An artifice or stratagem to be successful; to prevail by stratagem, &c.
(-ke), To bind the twists (of a turban) with care
adj. Twisted, convoluted, coiled, spiral; winding, sinuous; intricate, complicated, involved; knotty, difficult; ambiguous; crooked, tortuous; artful, tricky, wily, crafty, deceitful
adj. Coil within coil; involved, entangled, intricate, complex
(-ko), To wind round, to twist; to screw; to circumvent, to deceive
(-meṅ), To entangle (the string of an adversary's paper kite); to throw obstacles or difficulties (in the way of)
(-meṅ; or -se), To practise artifices or tricks, to trick, deceive; to complicate; — to grapple (with, in wrestling)
v.n. To be twisted, &c.; to coil; to writhe; to be perplexed; to fall into difficulties, to sustain a loss
or khul-jānā, v.n. To be untwisted; to be unscrewed
(-se); To entangle (the silk of one's paper kite with that of another kite), to fight paper kites; — to meet, to embrace
peć-tāb, s.m. Twisting and twining; convolution, twisting knots, folds; contortions; restlessness, anxiety, agitation, perplexity, disquietude, distraction, distress; vexation, anger, indignation
v.n. To twist and turn; to writhe; to suffer distress or anxiety; to be vexed.

def: s.m. An owl.
s.f. A ball, clue, or skein (of thread); — the bottom on which thread is wound.
s.m. An owl ( = pećā); — a louse; — the root of an elephant's tail; — a cloud; — a bed.
part. adj. Turning, twisting, winding, coiling, &c.; — twisted, coiled, &c.

ety: perf. part. of pećīdan, fr. peć, q.v.
def: part. adj. Twisted, coiled, &c.: See peć-dār, s.v. peć.
s.f. The being twisted; twisting, winding; twist, contortion; distortion.
s.f. Inflection, twisting, contortion, writhing; intricacy, perplexity, trouble; contention; circumvention; — tenesmus, cholic, gripes.
s.m. A species of bulbous plant with esculent roots, Colocasia antiquorum.
adj. Twisted (generally applied to ḥuqqa-snakes); — s.m. A ḥuqqa-snake.
s.m. = peṅḍālū, q.v.
s.f. A small basket; portmanteau; box, chest.
s.m. = (Old H.) پيکهن pekhaṇ, q.v.
s.m. Seeing, &c. (see next).
s.m. See the Hindī پيکهنا pekhnā (of which it is the Urdū form).

ety: pekh˚ = Prk. पेक्व(इ) = S. प्रेक्ष(ते), rt. ईक्ष् with प्र
def: v.t. To see, look, behold ( = dekhnā); to desire, wish for, long for.
s.m. A puppet-show; show, spectacle, play, comedy, farce, sham; illusion; — a plaything, toy; the wiles and artifices of woman.
s.m. Player, actor, &c.
s.m. See pelā.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian پیل (pīl). Cognate with Pashto پيل (pil).

def: s.m. Shove, push; — s.f. Crush, crowd; abundance, plenty (see rel; and rel-pel)
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian پیل (pīl). Cognate with Pashto پيل (pil).

def: s.m. A testicle ( = pelaṛ).
wety: Inherited from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀧𑀺𑀢𑁆𑀢𑀮 (pittala), from Sanskrit पीतल (pītala, “brass, yellow”).

def: s.m. A prop, support; — oppression, tyranny; wrong, fault.
wety: Inherited from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀧𑀺𑀢𑁆𑀢𑀮 (pittala), from Sanskrit पीतल (pītala, “brass, yellow”).

def: s.m. The ball or cocoon which the silk-worm forms round itself; the silkworm ( = kirm-pīla).

ety: Prk. पेलडं, पेलडअं; S. पेल+रं, and र+कं
def: s.m. A testicle.

ety: Prk. पेलडं, पेलडअं; S. पेल+रं, and र+कं
def: s.m. A testicle.
s.m. = pelaṛ, pelṛā, q.v. s.m. A testicle.
s.m. = pelaṛ, pelṛā, q.v. s.m. A testicle.

ety: pel˚ = Prk. पेल्ल(इ); S. प्रेरय(ति), rt. ईर् with प्र; or S. पीड(ते), rt. पीड्
def: v.t. To shove, push, thrust, jostle; to impel, drive on forcibly, to push on; to push down, thrust in; to press, crush, squeeze; to stuff, cram, ram; to express, squeeze out.

ety: Prk. पेलडं, पेलडअं; S. पेल+रं, and र+कं
def: s.m. A testicle.

ety: pel˚, rt. of pelnā, q.v.+ū = Prk. इउओ = S. (इ)तृ+कः
def: s.m. A pusher, shover; wrestler, athlete.
s.m. = prem, q.v. s.m. Love, affection, kindness, tender regard, kindliness, friendship
subs: adj. Devoted to the exercise of love
adj. & s.m. Intent on love, filled with affection, affectionate, loving, constant; — one who is constant in love, &c.
s.m. The quality or the essence of love
(-ke sāth), To be in love (with); to be fast friends (with)
adj. 'Coloured with the dye of love,' strongly attached (to), in love (with), enamoured (of)
s.m. 'Ocean of love'; the name of the tenth section of the Bhāgavat purāṇ, in which the love-sports of Kr̤ishṇa are related; an epithet of the Deity
adj. Connected with love, having relation to love
(-par), To show affection (for); to be enamoured (of)
s.f. A creeper; a parasitic plant
prem-vat, adj. Full of love, affectionate; — prem-vatī, s.f. Mistress, spouse
adj. Under the influence of love, over head and ears in love, deeply enamoured (of).

def: adj. & s.m. = premī, q.v. adj. & s.m. Loving, affectionate, friendly; — an affectionate person; a lover.
s.m. An imitative sound; singing, humming, buzzing; squeak, squeal; — peṅ, s.m. Pride, conceit

s.m. = peć, q.v. s.m. Turn, winding; revolution; involution; convolution; — twist, coil; plait, fold; — entanglement, complication, maze, perplexity, intricacy, ambiguity; hitch, difficulty, trouble; trick (in wrestling, &c.); artifice, stratagem; wiliness, craftiness; duplicity, deceit; — a roll (of cloth, &c.); a screw; a machine
subs: s.m. & adj. Twist upon twist, all convolution; all intricacy, &c.; — involved, intricate, &c.
v.n. To experience trouble, suffer distress or loss
v.n. The twist (of anything) to become loose; a screw to come out
(-meṅ), A difficulty or hitch to arise (in); to occur as a difficulty or obstacle; to become complicated, difficult, or intricate; — to be entangled (in)
v.n. An artifice or stratagem to be successful; to prevail by stratagem, &c.
(-ke), To bind the twists (of a turban) with care
adj. Twisted, convoluted, coiled, spiral; winding, sinuous; intricate, complicated, involved; knotty, difficult; ambiguous; crooked, tortuous; artful, tricky, wily, crafty, deceitful
adj. Coil within coil; involved, entangled, intricate, complex
(-ko), To wind round, to twist; to screw; to circumvent, to deceive
(-meṅ), To entangle (the string of an adversary's paper kite); to throw obstacles or difficulties (in the way of)
(-meṅ; or -se), To practise artifices or tricks, to trick, deceive; to complicate; — to grapple (with, in wrestling)
v.n. To be twisted, &c.; to coil; to writhe; to be perplexed; to fall into difficulties, to sustain a loss
or khul-jānā, v.n. To be untwisted; to be unscrewed
(-se); To entangle (the silk of one's paper kite with that of another kite), to fight paper kites; — to meet, to embrace
peć-tāb, s.m. Twisting and twining; convolution, twisting knots, folds; contortions; restlessness, anxiety, agitation, perplexity, disquietude, distraction, distress; vexation, anger, indignation
v.n. To twist and turn; to writhe; to suffer distress or anxiety; to be vexed.

def: s.m. A ball, lump, &c. (see pinḍ); rising ground, an eminence
subs: s.m. The sweet or spanish potato, Batatos paniculata (cf. pinḍālu, s.v. piṇḍ)

def: s.f. Step, pace; way, path, track (see paiṅḍā).
s.m. The bottom (of a vessel, box, pot, &c.); the breech (of a cannon).
s.f. The bottom, &c. ( = pendā)
subs: v.n. To go down bottom foremost; to flop down; — to be overcome, be subdued.

def: s.f. Swinging; the exertion made by two people in a swing when they keep the swing in motion without the aid of others; — the ropes of a swing; — a kind of bird, Gracula chattareah (Buch.)
subs: pīṅg lenā, v.n. To swing very high.

def: s.f. Tenderness, fondness, affection.
v.t. corr. of pahinna, q.v. v.t. See پہننا pahinnā.
s.m. A pencil (lead or slate).
s.f. A stated or fixed market (syn. hāṭ), a market; market-day.
s.m. A tree; plant; shrub
wety: Inherited from Proto-Indo-Aryan *peḍ-. Cognate with Punjabi پیڑ (peṛ).

def: s.m. A lump, &c. (see pinḍa; and cf. peṛā).
wety: Inherited from Proto-Indo-Aryan *peḍ-. Cognate with Punjabi پیڑ (peṛ).

def: s.f. A kind of musical instrument.
s.m. A ball or lump of leavened dough; — a kind of sweetmeat (made of curds).

s.f. The trunk of a tree; stubble (from which a second crop is obtained); the indigo plant after being once cut; a kind of betel leaf; a rate paid by cultivators to zamīndārs for the use of fruit trees.
s.f. A kind of sweetmeat ( = peṛā).
v.t. = peṛnā, q.v.
v.t. To press, squeeze, crush, grind; to rack.
s.m. The turquoise ( = pīroza).
s.m. The ocean; — the sun; fire.
s.m. The belly below the navel, the pubes; locus ubi exoritur pubes.
s.m. A turkey.
adj. & s.m. White, leprous; vile, ignoble; — leprosy.

ety: Old P., pes; Pehl. pesh; Z. para; S. para
def: s.m. Front, fore part; the front piece (in a native coat); (in Gram.) the vowel-point ُ (termed ẓamma by the Arabs); — adv. & postpn., In front (of, -ke), before (in time or place; — syn. āge); — adj. Promoted, advanced; respected; trusted; possessing influence
subs: adv. Previous (to, -ke), before; — pesh az īn, adv. Previous to this, before this; formerly, heretofore ( = is-se āge, or is-se pahle)
s.f. Coming into the presence (of, kī), advance; access, admittance; advancement, elevation

v.n. To come before, step forward, to advance; to present (itself), to intervene, to arise, occur, happen, come to pass; to behave (with, -se, e.g. gustāḵẖī-se pesh āyā, 'he behaved insolently'), to treat; to deal (with), negotiate
adj. Looking to the future, provident, prudent
s.m. The belt that passes over a horse's breast to prevent the saddle's slipping back; a martingal
pesh ćalnā, v.n. To go forth, to advance; to gain reception (from, se), to pass, to answer; to be effective, have effect
s.m. A tent or other baggage sent on before; the camp equipage, tents, &c., sent on in advance of an army; — fore-runner, harbinger
s.m. Vestibule, porch, portico; verandah; gallery

adj. & s.m. Surpassing, excelling; outstripping; being beforehand, anticipating; — one who excels; an assistant; money paid in advance, earnest-money; — pesh-dastī, s.f. An outstripping, surpassing, excelling; pre-eminence, precedency, excellence; anticipation, the being beforehand (in, -meṅ), forestalling; paying beforehand; — pesh-dastī karnā, v.n. To make haste; to take the lead (in, -meṅ); to be beforehand (in), to anticipate
adj. & s.m. Early, first (fruit, grain, &c.); — first-fruits

adj. Making impression, impressive, forcible; successful, efficient, effectual (as a speech, admonition, &c.); — pesh-raft jānā, pesh-raft honā, v.n. To have effect, be effectual, produce an impression, to be heeded; to be successful, to succeed; — to take the lead, to go ahead
pesh-rau, s.m. One who goes before; leader; guide; precursor, fore-runner; party in advance; advance guard, van
s.f. The act of going before or preceding
s.f. Stepping before or forwards, advancing, advance; outstripping; anticipation; activity, alertness; aggression; — leadership, command
v.n. To go before, precede, go in advance; to advance, push forward; to outstrip; to anticipate; to take the first step, to commence; to be the aggressor
s.m. Deputy; manager, agent, foreman; assistant, mate; a native officer in a court next below the sarishtadār
s.f. Deputyship; agency, &c.; the office of a pesh-kār
v.t. To put or set before; to offer, present; to bring forward, adduce, submit, propose, represent
s.m. lit. 'What is first drawn'; first-fruits; — a present to a superior; a present to the ruling power on receiving an appointment, or on the renewal of a grant, lease, &c.
s.f. The foremost place, the front; vestibule, portico
s.m. A leader at prayer, an imām
s.m. lit. 'What is placed before'; exemplar, example, model; custom, habit, use; mode, manner; rule, regulation.

r: pesha, pesh, lit, pesha-wār, pesha-war, pesha-wālā
def: s.m. An artisan, craftsman, artificer; workman; tradesman.
s.m. Urine, piss
subs: peshāb-band, s.m. Stoppage or suppression of urine, strangury; — peshāb band honā, v.n. To suffer retention or stoppage of urine
v.n. To have the desire to pass urine, to want to piss
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian پیشاب (pēšāb, literally “front-water”).

def: s.m. Grinding, crushing, pulverizing; — any apparatus for grinding or pounding, a hand-mill; a stone and muller; a threshing-floor; — a plant, a species of Euphorbia with three lobes, Euphorbia antiquorum (com. called tekāṅtā-sij).
s.f. The forehead, front, brow; fate, lot, destiny (as traceable in the lines of the forehead).
s.f. A stone slab on which condiments, &c. are ground with a muller; mill, handmill; grindstone; millstone.
s.m. Name of a city on the north-west frontier of India (lit. 'an advanced post'; it was built by the emperor Akbar).
adj. & s.f. Of or belonging to Peshaur, coming from or made in Peshaur; — a kind of slipper or shoe made in Peshaur; — a kind of rice.
s.f. Money given in advance, or on account; an advance (of money); earnest-money.
s.f. The being put before, coming before (as a case before a judge), the being brought forward, or into the presence of; — lead, precedence; superior rank; — an advance ( = peshgī).
adj. Former, prior, anterior, ancient; anticipated; — s.m. The morning
s.m. pl. Those who have gone-before, the ancients.
= P پيشکش pesh-kash, See s.v. pesh.
= P پيشکار pesh-kār, See s.v. pesh.
adv. Before, prior (to, -ke, or -se); formerly; sooner; heretofore; ago; further; beyond.
s.m. Exemplar, model; guide, leader; foreman; the head of a party; high-priest; the chief of a religious sect; a moral or religious instructor; — the first executive officer (among the Marhaṭṭas)
subs: v.t. To follow as a model; to take as a guide, &c.
(-kā), To meet and receive (a friend or visitor).

def: s.f. Leadership; high-priestship; guidance; precedence, supremacy; the meeting and reception of a guest or visitor
subs: (-kī), To meet, receive, welcome; to escort.

def: s.f. A full-dress gown (reaching a little below the knee, — especially such as is worn by a bride when she leaves for her husband's home, and also by dancing girls).
s.f. A piece, ball, or lump of flesh or meat; a sheath, scabbard; the egg of a bird; spikenard, Valeriana jatamansi; a blown bud.
adj. Leprous.
adv. For the sake of the stomach
subs: peṭ-ārthī, adj. & s.m. Desirous for the stomach; greedy, gluttonous; epicurean; — a glutton; an epicure
v.n. To eat less than the appetite demands, to stint oneself of food
v.n. To eat voraciously; — to encroach on the share or rights of another
v.n. The stomach to rumble; to suffer from borborygm
adj. (f. -ī), Having the stomach full; satisfied; — rich, wealthy, well off, independent; consequential, proud, conceited
adj. & s.m. Enough to fill the stomach; a bellyful
v.n. To fill the belly; to be satisfied; to be surfeited
v.n. To pamper the stomach; to be selfish; — to contrive to live decently; to live from hand to mouth
adj. & s.m. Gluttonous, greedy, selfish; — a glutton; a selfish person
v.n. To have loose bowels; to be violently purged; to have watery stools; — to be white-livered, be frightened; to be confounded
v.n. lit. 'To hold the stomach and run'; to fly in great agitation or distress
v.n. lit. 'To walk about holding the stomach'; to wander about in a state of agitation or distraction, to be distracted
peṭ-posūʼā, adj. & s.m. Gluttonous, greedy; a glutton ( = peṭ-ārthu; peṭ-pālū)
peṭ-poćhan, peṭ-poṅćan, s.m. A woman's last child
v.n. To rend or tear the bowels; — to be impatient
v.n. The belly to burst; — to be impatient; to be bursting with envy, &c.
v.n. The belly to swell; to be in the family way; to be bursting (with laughter, &c.)
v.n. To beat the belly; to cry out with hunger; — to evince great impatience
(or peṭ-piṭh) batānā, v.n. To evince extreme weakness or want; to act submissively
(or peṭ piṭh) ek honā, v.n. lit. 'The belly and back to meet'; to be very emaciated
peṭ jhaṛnā, peṭ ćalnā, peṭ ćhūṭnā, v.n. To be purged; to have a diarrhœa or flux ( = peṭ pānī honā)
v.n. lit. 'To shew the belly'; to complain of poverty and hunger; to beg for food
v.n. To procure abortion; bring on a miscarriage
v.t. To get with child, cause to be pregnant
v.t. To bind (food, &c.) to the belly
v.n. One's teeth or claws to become visible; to shew one's claws; — to go beyond bounds
peṭ-se īṅṭ bāṅdhnā, peṭ-se kāṭ-kī roṭī bāṅdhnā, v.n. To bind a tight bandage, or a brick, or a cake-shaped board, to the stomach to withstand the craving of hunger
v.n. To be carried (in a conveyance), or to ride, without being jolted

(-kā), To starve or pinch (oneself); to deprive (one) of dues, livelihood, &c.; to stop the wages (of); to reduce the allowance or stipend (of); — peṭ-kā ṭūṭā, adj. Starving, famished, famine-stricken
s.m. One subject to pain in the stomach (from over-feeding); — a destitute or starved person, a poor wretch; — a wretch who will do anything for the sake of his stomach
peṭ-kā kuttā, adj. As a hog, or a dog, in respect of (his) stomach; greedy as a hog, &c.
s.m. One who works for bare subsistence; a hireling, mercenary
adj. (f. -ī), Light of stomach; light, frivolous; gossiping, babbling; unable to keep a secret
s.f. Fire of the stomach; hunger; — maternal affection
v.n. To appease hunger; — peṭ-kī angār, s.f. Violent craving of the stomach (for food); — violent emotion
peṭ-ke pāʼoṅ bāhar nikālnā = peṭ-se pāʼoṅ bāhar nikālnā
adv. For the sake of the stomach; for food
(-kā), To cause or procure abortion ( = peṭ ḍalnā)
v.n. To miscarry (a woman); — s.m. Miscarriage
peṭ guṛgurānā, v.n. To have a rumbling of the stomach ( = peṭ bolnā, q.v.)
v.n. The stomach to sink in from want of food; to be starving with hunger
(apnā), To subdue (one's) appetite or hunger; to mortify (oneself); to stab (oneself) in the belly; — to screen, cloak, hide, excuse (a child from maternal affection)
s.f. A woman who works for her daily bread; a woman who prostitutes herself for bread or for money; a prostitute, a harlot
v.n. To have the stomach rubbed
v.n. To have a twisting pain in the stomach (from excessive laughter, &c.)
v.n. To keep a thing, &c. to oneself; — peṭ-meṅ pānī honā, v.n. = peṭ pānī honā, q.v.
v.n. To be very sly; to entertain evil designs
v.n. To get into the stomach (of, -ke); — to pass the lips (of); — to conceive
(-ke), To worm oneself into the secrets (of); to worm oneself into the good graces (of); to become intimate (with)
v.n. To have an old head on young shoulders
(-ko), To stow away in the stomach; to keep (a matter) secret, or to oneself; to keep back; to suppress
v.t. To gulp down; to endure, to have patience
v.n. To have a griping pain in the stomach, to have an inclination to stool.
wety: Borrowed from Sanskrit पेट (peṭa, “bag, basket”), from the root verb पिट् (piṭ, “to collect or heap together”).

def: s.m. Belly; round, girth; breadth, width, diameter, distance across; slackness or curvature (of a string or rope hung loosely between two points); the middle (of a page); the channel (of a river); a round or total sum; — adv. In round numbers, about.
s.f. A basket; a large basket.
s.m. A covered wicker-basket for holding clothes, books, &c.; — a multitude.
adj. Big-bellied, gorbellied.
s.m. See piṭārā.
adj. & s.m. See s.v. peṭ. adv. For the sake of the stomach
subs: peṭ-ārthī, adj. & s.m. Desirous for the stomach; greedy, gluttonous; epicurean; — a glutton; an epicure
v.n. To eat less than the appetite demands, to stint oneself of food
v.n. To eat voraciously; — to encroach on the share or rights of another
v.n. The stomach to rumble; to suffer from borborygm
adj. (f. -ī), Having the stomach full; satisfied; — rich, wealthy, well off, independent; consequential, proud, conceited
adj. & s.m. Enough to fill the stomach; a bellyful
v.n. To fill the belly; to be satisfied; to be surfeited
v.n. To pamper the stomach; to be selfish; — to contrive to live decently; to live from hand to mouth
adj. & s.m. Gluttonous, greedy, selfish; — a glutton; a selfish person
v.n. To have loose bowels; to be violently purged; to have watery stools; — to be white-livered, be frightened; to be confounded
v.n. lit. 'To hold the stomach and run'; to fly in great agitation or distress
v.n. lit. 'To walk about holding the stomach'; to wander about in a state of agitation or distraction, to be distracted
peṭ-posūʼā, adj. & s.m. Gluttonous, greedy; a glutton ( = peṭ-ārthu; peṭ-pālū)
peṭ-poćhan, peṭ-poṅćan, s.m. A woman's last child
v.n. To rend or tear the bowels; — to be impatient
v.n. The belly to burst; — to be impatient; to be bursting with envy, &c.
v.n. The belly to swell; to be in the family way; to be bursting (with laughter, &c.)
v.n. To beat the belly; to cry out with hunger; — to evince great impatience
(or peṭ-piṭh) batānā, v.n. To evince extreme weakness or want; to act submissively
(or peṭ piṭh) ek honā, v.n. lit. 'The belly and back to meet'; to be very emaciated
peṭ jhaṛnā, peṭ ćalnā, peṭ ćhūṭnā, v.n. To be purged; to have a diarrhœa or flux ( = peṭ pānī honā)
v.n. lit. 'To shew the belly'; to complain of poverty and hunger; to beg for food
v.n. To procure abortion; bring on a miscarriage
v.t. To get with child, cause to be pregnant
v.t. To bind (food, &c.) to the belly
v.n. One's teeth or claws to become visible; to shew one's claws; — to go beyond bounds
peṭ-se īṅṭ bāṅdhnā, peṭ-se kāṭ-kī roṭī bāṅdhnā, v.n. To bind a tight bandage, or a brick, or a cake-shaped board, to the stomach to withstand the craving of hunger
v.n. To be carried (in a conveyance), or to ride, without being jolted

(-kā), To starve or pinch (oneself); to deprive (one) of dues, livelihood, &c.; to stop the wages (of); to reduce the allowance or stipend (of); — peṭ-kā ṭūṭā, adj. Starving, famished, famine-stricken
s.m. One subject to pain in the stomach (from over-feeding); — a destitute or starved person, a poor wretch; — a wretch who will do anything for the sake of his stomach
peṭ-kā kuttā, adj. As a hog, or a dog, in respect of (his) stomach; greedy as a hog, &c.
s.m. One who works for bare subsistence; a hireling, mercenary
adj. (f. -ī), Light of stomach; light, frivolous; gossiping, babbling; unable to keep a secret
s.f. Fire of the stomach; hunger; — maternal affection
v.n. To appease hunger; — peṭ-kī angār, s.f. Violent craving of the stomach (for food); — violent emotion
peṭ-ke pāʼoṅ bāhar nikālnā = peṭ-se pāʼoṅ bāhar nikālnā
adv. For the sake of the stomach; for food
(-kā), To cause or procure abortion ( = peṭ ḍalnā)
v.n. To miscarry (a woman); — s.m. Miscarriage
peṭ guṛgurānā, v.n. To have a rumbling of the stomach ( = peṭ bolnā, q.v.)
v.n. The stomach to sink in from want of food; to be starving with hunger
(apnā), To subdue (one's) appetite or hunger; to mortify (oneself); to stab (oneself) in the belly; — to screen, cloak, hide, excuse (a child from maternal affection)
s.f. A woman who works for her daily bread; a woman who prostitutes herself for bread or for money; a prostitute, a harlot
v.n. To have the stomach rubbed
v.n. To have a twisting pain in the stomach (from excessive laughter, &c.)
v.n. To keep a thing, &c. to oneself; — peṭ-meṅ pānī honā, v.n. = peṭ pānī honā, q.v.
v.n. To be very sly; to entertain evil designs
v.n. To get into the stomach (of, -ke); — to pass the lips (of); — to conceive
(-ke), To worm oneself into the secrets (of); to worm oneself into the good graces (of); to become intimate (with)
v.n. To have an old head on young shoulders
(-ko), To stow away in the stomach; to keep (a matter) secret, or to oneself; to keep back; to suppress
v.t. To gulp down; to endure, to have patience
v.n. To have a griping pain in the stomach, to have an inclination to stool.

def: s.m. A kind of gourd; a kind of sweetmeat, slices of the gourd candied.
s.f. dim. of, and = peṅṭh, q.v. s.f. A stated or fixed market (syn. hāṭ), a market; market-day.
s.f. Belly-band, girth, belt; portmanteau; box, tumbrel, casket; bundle, case (of things); the thorax, chest
subs: peṭī mārnā (-se), To have sexual intercourse (with).

def: s.f. A box.
s.m. Daily food, daily allowance, board; allowance, pension.
s.m. Looseness of the bowels, purging, flux, diarrhœa.
adj. & s.m. Gluttonous, voracious; — a glutton, gourmand.
adj. & s.m. = peṭū, q.v. adj. & s.m. Gluttonous, voracious; — a glutton, gourmand.
s.f. A pregnant woman.
s.f. Yellow chalk; yellow colour (said to be obtained from the urine of a cow fed on the flowers of Butea frondosa, or, according to others, on the leaves of the mango tree).
s.f. Biestings ( = pīyūs, q.v.). s.m. The milk of a cow during the first seven days after calving, biestings; any thick fluid, cream, juice; — the food of the gods, ambrosia, nectar.
adj. & s.m. Suitable or fit to be taken or drunk, drinkable; — water, milk, any drink.
part. adj. Sifting (used in comp.).
s.m. A sieve (syn. ćālnī).

r: pha, Nāgarī, p, ph, loop-hole, pha-kār
ety: sounded nearly like the ph in the English word loop-hole
def: s.m. The letter or sound ph.
s.f. = phaban, q.v. s.f. Embellishment, ornament, decoration, dress; elegance, grace, charm, beauty, comeliness, loveliness.
part. adj. = phabtā, q.v. part. adj. (f. -ī), Becoming, fit, suitable, proper, pertinent; — phabte, adv. Fittingly, pertinently, àpropos.
v.n. To shoot forth, to flourish (as plants; — cf. bhabaknā).
s.f. Embellishment, ornament, decoration, dress; elegance, grace, charm, beauty, comeliness, loveliness.
v.n. = phabnā, q.v. v.n. To become, befit, be fitting, fit, suit; to be pertinent; — to be an embellishment or ornament (to), to grace, adorn; to look elegant, graceful, beautiful, &c.
adj. (Old H.) = phabīlā, q.v. part. adj. (f. -ī), Becoming, fit, suitable, proper, pertinent; — phabte, adv. Fittingly, pertinently, àpropos.
adj. (f. -ī), Becoming, fit, &c. = phabtā, q.v. part. adj. (f. -ī), Becoming, fit, suitable, proper, pertinent; — phabte, adv. Fittingly, pertinently, àpropos.
v.n. To become, befit, be fitting, fit, suit; to be pertinent; — to be an embellishment or ornament (to), to grace, adorn; to look elegant, graceful, beautiful, &c.
part. adj. (f. -ī), Becoming, fit, suitable, proper, pertinent; — phabte, adv. Fittingly, pertinently, àpropos.
s.f. Ornament, decoration; — conjecturing what a person is by his dress; — pleasantry, fun, jest; a name given in jest (which sticks to one)
subs: v.n. To make fun, to jest: phabtī kahnā, v.n. To utter a jest, &c. = phabtī uṛānā; — to say or conjecture by one's dress what sort of person he is or to what caste he belongs.

def: v.n. To bubble, to fizz, to sputter; to ferment; to shoot, throw out shoots (as a plant); to break out (as an eruption); to be inflamed (with lust).
s.m. A red powder thrown over one another by the Hindūs at the holī festival; the act of throwing coloured powders at the time of the Holī; the sports of the Holī
subs: v.n. To take part in the gambols of the Holī, to play holī.

def: s.m. The eleventh month of the Hindūs (Feb.-March, the full moon of which is near Pūrvā-phālgunī).
s.f. (prov.) = phāg.
s.f. = phāg, q.v. s.m. A red powder thrown over one another by the Hindūs at the holī festival; the act of throwing coloured powders at the time of the Holī; the sports of the Holī
subs: v.n. To take part in the gambols of the Holī, to play holī.

def: s.m. The Holī festival; the song of the Holī festival; presents made during the Holī.
s.m. A flock of cotton (used for scenting, or as lint to dress a wound); a plaster, pledget (syn. phūhā or phoyā, q.v.).
v.n. To fly (as a flag), to wave, flutter ( = pharrānā, q.v.). v.n. To flutter, flap, wave (as a flag, a bird, &c.); to whiz, whir, whistle; to snort (as a horse).
v.t. To cause (a flag) to wave or fly; to wave, to fly, to flap (a flag).

ety: caus. of phailnā; — phailā˚ = phail+ā = āw = Prk. आव or आवे = S. आपि caus. increment
def: v.t. To stretch, stretch out, extend, expand; to open wide, distend, dilate; to enlarge, increase; to lengthen, draw out, deploy; to spread, spread out; to scatter, strew; to diffuse, spread abroad, publish, proclaim, propagate; to spread over, to distribute, apportion; to work out (a sum), to compute, calculate.

ety: phailā˚, rt. of phailānā+Prk. एअव्वअं+ट्ट; = S. इतव्य+कं+ त्वं
def: Spreading, spreading out; extension, expansion, distension, enlargement, increase, growth; extent, length, breadth; spread, diffusion, publication, propagation; display; abundance, plenty, profusion; prolixity; distribution, apportionment; — working (of a sum), calculation, computation.

ety: phailā˚, rt. of phailānā+Prk. एअव्वअं+ट्ट; = S. इतव्य+कं+ त्वं
def: Spreading, spreading out; extension, expansion, distension, enlargement, increase, growth; extent, length, breadth; spread, diffusion, publication, propagation; display; abundance, plenty, profusion; prolixity; distribution, apportionment; — working (of a sum), calculation, computation.

ety: phailā˚, rt. of phailānā+Prk. एअव्वअं+ट्ट; = S. इतव्य+कं+ त्वं
def: Spreading, spreading out; extension, expansion, distension, enlargement, increase, growth; extent, length, breadth; spread, diffusion, publication, propagation; display; abundance, plenty, profusion; prolixity; distribution, apportionment; — working (of a sum), calculation, computation.

ety: phail˚ = Prk. फेल(इ) = फेड(इ) or फेडे(इ) = S. स्फेटय(ति), rt. स्फिदः and फेल(ति), rt. फेल् adopted fr. Prk
def: v.n. To be spread, be expanded, be dilated; to be diffused, be scattered, be dispersed; to spread, expand, extend, stretch or reach out; to grow, increase (in size, &c.); to spread abroad, become public; to rise in one's demands or requirements, be exacting; to become pressing, to insist, persist
s.m. = phāg; and phāṅk, qq.v. s.m. A red powder thrown over one another by the Hindūs at the holī festival; the act of throwing coloured powders at the time of the Holī; the sports of the Holī
subs: v.n. To take part in the gambols of the Holī, to play holī.

def: s.m. Sound, noise
subs: phak-denī, adv. In a moment, all-at-once; suddenly.

def: s.f. = phaṅkī; phāṅkī, qq.v. s.f. A mouthful of dry grain, powder, &c.; a piece or slice of fruit.
s.m. = phāṅkā, q.v. s.m. As much of anything as can be thrown or taken into the mouth; bit, piece, slice (of fruit), flake; a clove (of garlic); — cut, opening, aperture (in fruit, &c.)
subs: v.t. To chuck (grain, &c.), into the mouth ( = phāṅknā).

def: s.m. Raillery, indecent or abusive language, abuse; low and coarse jesting
subs: s.m. One who indulges in low and obscene language, an indecent prater; one who is given to scurrilous abuse.
